question: What powers India?

theme.label: Energy and electricity
readerQuestions: How much energy or electricity is being used?
Is access different from reliability or consumption?
Is the system getting cleaner as demand grows?

visualPlan.title: Electricity generation by source
visualPlan.recommendedVisualization: stacked mix bar plus selected source trends
visualPlan.mobileGuidance: Use a stacked bar for the latest mix and avoid too many fuel lines on mobile.
visualPlan.whyThisMatters: This is the chart that answers the page question most directly.

plannedCharts.title: Electricity is the visible layer
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Most readers feel the energy system through electricity: coal still dominates generation, while clean sources are growing.

plannedCharts.title: Coal, clean power, and gas generation
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The central power-system chart: it shows the absolute work done by coal versus clean generation.

plannedCharts.title: Crude oil imports in 2025-26
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: PPAC is the Indian petroleum source; it shows the monthly crude import flow in the latest available fiscal-year report.

plannedCharts.title: What India pays per barrel
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The Indian crude basket price translates global oil markets into the price India actually pays for imported crude.

plannedCharts.title: Fuel import bill in dollars
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: This shows the money scale of India's imported fuels: crude dominates, but coal, products, and LNG are material.

plannedCharts.title: Fuel import bill in rupees
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The rupee view makes the import bill legible in Indian fiscal terms.

plannedCharts.title: Where India's crude oil imports come from
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: TradeStat/DGCI&S gives the official India-side partner table for HS 270900 crude oil in 2025-26.

plannedCharts.title: How Russia became a major crude supplier
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The current partner chart needs history: Russia was not a major source in 2017-18, then became India's largest crude supplier after 2022.

plannedCharts.title: Where India's coal imports come from
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Coal still powers the grid, and TradeStat shows which countries supply imported coal.

plannedCharts.title: Where India's LNG imports come from
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Gas is smaller than coal and oil, but LNG creates another named import channel.

plannedCharts.title: LNG import volumes in 2025-26
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: PPAC's gas page gives the physical monthly LNG import flow, complementing TradeStat's partner-country value table.

plannedCharts.title: Where petroleum product imports come from
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: India imports not only crude but also selected petroleum products, so this shows the product side of the oil trade.

plannedCharts.title: The grid is getting cleaner per unit
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The transition is visible in electricity carbon intensity, even if total energy use keeps rising.

plannedCharts.title: Electricity demand keeps climbing
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Clean power has to grow against rising demand, not a fixed electricity system.

selectionRules: Start from the page question, not from the indicator name.
Choose the smallest set of visuals that explains scale, trend, structure, comparison, and caveat.
Prefer mobile-friendly charts: line charts with sparse ticks, horizontal bars, share strips, pyramids only when essential, and no dense tables.
Use one strong pull quote only when the evidence supports it with a number.
Add glossary blocks for technical terms that normal readers may not know.
caveats: Use only figures present in lockedNumbers or sourceSummaries.
If a related theme indicator is present, you may use it for context, but do not pretend it is the main answer.
If source coverage is thin, say so directly.
Do not compare states, districts, or cities unless the evidence packet contains those geographies.
forbiddenClaims: Do not infer causality from a trend unless the evidence explicitly supports it.
Do not cite a source that is not present in sourceSummaries.
